user.title: Kubernetes Configuration
user.description: Enter your Kubernetes cluster connection details. When Home Assistant is running inside the Kubernetes cluster, the host, port, API token, and CA certificate fields are pre-filled from the pod's ServiceAccount.

data_description.cluster_name: A friendly name to identify this Kubernetes cluster in Home Assistant. This name will be used as the integration name and for device identification.
data_description.host: The hostname or IP address of your Kubernetes API server. Do not include the protocol (http:// or https://).
data_description.port: The port number for the Kubernetes API server.
data_description.api_token: A valid Kubernetes service account token with appropriate RBAC permissions. This token is used to authenticate API requests to your cluster. It is required to access the Kubernetes API.
data_description.ca_cert: Optional path to your cluster's CA certificate file. Required if using self-signed certificates or custom Certificate Authorities. Leave empty if using standard certificates.
data_description.verify_ssl: Whether to verify SSL certificates when connecting to the Kubernetes API. Disable this only for self-signed certificates or development environments.
data_description.use_in_cluster: When Home Assistant runs inside the Kubernetes cluster, re-read the bearer token from the projected ServiceAccount volume on each request. This handles automatic token rotation. The token entered above is kept as a fallback if the file becomes unreadable.
data_description.monitor_all_namespaces: When enabled, the integration will monitor all namespaces in your cluster. When disabled, you will be prompted to select specific namespaces to monitor. Requires cluster-wide permissions when enabled.
data_description.switch_update_interval: How often (in seconds) the integration polls the Kubernetes API to update switch states. Lower values provide more responsive switches but increase API load.
data_description.scale_verification_timeout: Maximum time (in seconds) to wait for scaling operations to complete before timing out. Increase this value for slow clusters or large deployments.
data_description.scale_cooldown: Cooldown period (in seconds) after scaling operations before allowing another scale operation. This prevents rapid successive scaling requests.
data_description.device_grouping_mode: Choose how entities are organized in Home Assistant. Group by Namespace creates a device for each namespace, while Group by Cluster creates a single device containing all nodes, sensors, and entities.

namespaces.title: Select Namespaces to Monitor
namespaces.description:
Choose which Kubernetes namespaces you want to monitor. You can select multiple namespaces from the list below. Only resources in the selected namespaces will be monitored by this integration.

**Tip:** Use the dropdown to search and select namespaces. Selected namespaces will appear as chips that can be removed by clicking the X icon.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Kubernetes Connection
reconfigure.description: Update your Kubernetes cluster connection details and settings. The cluster name cannot be changed.

data_description.host: The hostname or IP address of your Kubernetes API server. Do not include the protocol (http:// or https://).
data_description.port: The port number for the Kubernetes API server.
data_description.api_token: A valid Kubernetes service account token with appropriate RBAC permissions.
data_description.ca_cert: Optional path to your cluster's CA certificate file. Leave empty if using standard certificates.
data_description.verify_ssl: Whether to verify SSL certificates when connecting to the Kubernetes API.
data_description.use_in_cluster: When Home Assistant runs inside the Kubernetes cluster, re-read the bearer token from the projected ServiceAccount volume on each request. This handles automatic token rotation. The token entered above is kept as a fallback if the file becomes unreadable.
data_description.monitor_all_namespaces: When enabled, the integration will monitor all namespaces in your cluster. When disabled, you will be prompted to select specific namespaces to monitor.
data_description.switch_update_interval: How often (in seconds) the integration polls the Kubernetes API to update switch states.
data_description.scale_verification_timeout: Maximum time (in seconds) to wait for scaling operations to complete before timing out.
data_description.scale_cooldown: Cooldown period (in seconds) after scaling operations before allowing another scale operation.
data_description.device_grouping_mode: Choose how entities are organized in Home Assistant.

reconfigure_namespaces.title: Select Namespaces to Monitor
reconfigure_namespaces.description: Choose which Kubernetes namespaces you want to monitor. Only resources in the selected namespaces will be monitored.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to Kubernetes cluster
error.cannot_fetch_namespaces: Failed to fetch namespaces from cluster. Please check your connection settings and permissions.
error.kubernetes_not_installed: Kubernetes Python package is not installed. Please install it using: pip install kubernetes==33.1.0
error.at_least_one_namespace_required: At least one namespace is required when not monitoring all namespaces

init.title: Kubernetes Options
init.description: Configure advanced options for the Kubernetes integration.

data_description.enable_panel: Show the Kubernetes dashboard panel in the Home Assistant sidebar. The panel provides an overview of your cluster, nodes, pods, workloads, and integration settings. Disable this if you prefer to use only the standard Home Assistant entities and services.
data_description.enable_watch: ⚠️ Experimental: Use the Kubernetes watch API for real-time state updates instead of polling. When enabled, pod and resource state changes appear in Home Assistant within seconds. Polling continues every 5 minutes as a fallback. Requires the service account to have 'watch' permission on all monitored resources.
data_description.enable_events: Create a 'Cluster events' event entity that fires Home Assistant events for Kubernetes cluster activity (OOMKilling, FailedScheduling, BackOff, Evicted, Unhealthy, ImagePullBackOff, and more). Use these events to trigger automations and alerts. Requires get/list/watch permission on core v1 events (granted by manifests/full/).
data_description.event_types: Which Kubernetes event types to surface. 'Warning only' (default) limits events to Warning-type Kubernetes events such as OOM kills and scheduling failures. 'All events' also includes Normal-type informational events. Note: recurring conditions (e.g. CrashLoopBackOff) fire an event on each Kubernetes count increment.

delete_job.name: Delete Job
delete_job.description: Delete one or more Kubernetes Jobs (also deletes their pods). Useful for clearing failed Jobs.

job_name.name: Job Name
job_name.description: The name of a single Job to delete (use job_names for multiple).

job_names.name: Job Names
job_names.description: A list of Job names to delete (use job_name for a single Job).

namespace.name: Namespace
namespace.description: The namespace of the Job(s). If omitted, it is resolved from monitored data, then the configured default.

entry_id.name: Config Entry ID
entry_id.description: Target a specific configured cluster (optional; defaults to the first).

kubernetes_package_missing.title: Kubernetes Python package is not installed
kubernetes_package_missing.description: The integration requires the official `kubernetes` Python package, which is normally installed automatically. If you see this message, your Home Assistant installation is missing the dependency. Restart Home Assistant or reinstall the integration to retry.

metrics_server_unavailable.title: Metrics server unavailable for {cluster}
metrics_server_unavailable.description: The cluster `{cluster}` did not return CPU/memory metrics. Either the metrics-server addon is not installed, or the integration's ClusterRole is missing `get`/`list` permissions on the `metrics.k8s.io` API. Install metrics-server (see the Learn more link) or grant the additional RBAC permissions. This message clears automatically once metrics are returned.

watch_connection_failing.title: Watch connection failing for {cluster}
watch_connection_failing.description: The integration's watch/event-stream connection to cluster `{cluster}` has failed repeatedly and is retrying with backoff (used by the experimental Watch API and the cluster event stream). Live updates may be delayed while the connection retries with backoff; the integration continues periodic polling in the meantime. Check that the cluster API is reachable and that the ServiceAccount still has `watch` permission. This message clears automatically once the watch reconnects.
